title: Air-fryer chicken tenders
subtitle: Breaded chicken strips without a deep-fryer
excerpt: Chicken breast strips coated in seasoned flour, egg, and panko breadcrumbs, then cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 12 minutes. The hot circulating air crisps the panko coating on all sides without any oil beyond a light spray. A spray of oil on the coated tenders is the single step that makes the difference between pale and golden.
sourceNotes: Breaded chicken strips or tenders are an American fast-food staple that became popular in British home cooking via takeaway menus and air-fryer recipe books. The method replicates the deep-fried restaurant version using an oil spray to produce the crisp panko coating without immersion in fat. Panko breadcrumbs are preferred over standard dried breadcrumbs because their coarser, drier texture produces a crunchier result.

content.text: Spray the coated tenders with oil immediately before they go into the air fryer. The panko needs a film of fat to go golden: without it, the breadcrumbs remain pale and powdery no matter how long they cook. A two-second spray with a cooking oil spray is all it takes.

Set up three shallow bowls: one with the seasoned {{plain-flour}}, one with the beaten {{eggs}}, and one with the {{breadcrumbs-panko}} mixed with {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Working one at a time, coat each strip of {{chicken-breast}} in flour (shake off excess), dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko so every surface is covered. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange the tenders in a single layer in the basket. Spray generously with cooking oil spray: every crumb should glisten. Cook at 200°C for 12 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden all over and the chicken is cooked through.

items.fix: Ensure the strip is fully coated in egg before pressing into panko. Press the crumbs firmly rather than just rolling the strip through them. Place on a wire rack for 5 minutes to set before air frying
items.cause: Not enough egg as a binder, or coating applied too loosely

items.fix: The oil spray is the critical step. Without it, the panko cannot undergo the Maillard reaction and will stay pale. Spray from about 15 cm away for even coverage
items.cause: No oil spray applied before cooking

items.fix: Spray the basket itself before placing the tenders. Preheating for 3 minutes also prevents sticking. Silicone liners can help but they reduce air circulation and will produce a less crisp result
items.cause: Basket not sprayed with oil, or basket not preheated

content.text: Air-fryer chicken tenders are one of the most popular uses of the appliance in British households with children. Serve with ketchup, BBQ sauce, honey mustard, or hot sauce. Freeze the breaded uncooked tenders in a single layer and cook from frozen for a fast meal.
